contents.userQuery:
# Add CLI Commands for Export/Import in app/Main.hs

## Overview
Add CLI commands to `app/Main.hs` for exporting and importing agent configurations via archives or git repositories.

## Background
The existing CLI structure in `app/Main.hs`:
```haskell
data Command
    = Check
    | TerminalUI TuiOptions
    | OneShot OneShotOptions
    | EchoPrompt OneShotOptions
    | SelfDescribe
    | Initialize
    | McpServer
    | SessionPrint SessionPrint.SessionPrintOptions
```

Commands are parsed using `optparse-applicative` and added to the subparser in `parseProgOptions`.

## Requirements

### 1. Extend Command Type
Add new constructors to the `Command` type:

```haskell
data Command
    = Check
    | TerminalUI TuiOptions
    | OneShot OneShotOptions
    | EchoPrompt OneShotOptions
    | SelfDescribe
    | Initialize
    | McpServer
    | SessionPrint SessionPrint.SessionPrintOptions
    | Export ExportOptions   -- NEW
    | Import ImportOptions   -- NEW
```

### 2. Define Export Options
```haskell
data ExportOptions = ExportOptions
    { exportSource :: ExportSource        -- What to export
    , exportDestination :: ExportDestination  -- Where to export
    , exportFormat :: Maybe ArchiveFormat -- Override format detection
    , exportNamespace :: Maybe Text       -- Add namespace prefix
    , exportIncludeTools :: Bool          -- Include tool scripts
    , exportIncludeMcp :: Bool            -- Include MCP configs
    }

data ExportSource
    = ExportCurrentAgent                  -- Export agent from --agent-file
    | ExportAllAgents                     -- Export all loaded agents
    | ExportAgentBySlug Text              -- Export specific agent by slug

data ExportDestination
    = ExportToFile FilePath               -- Archive file path
    | ExportToGit GitExportOptions        -- Git repository

data GitExportOptions = GitExportOptions
    { gitUrl :: Text
    , gitBranch :: Maybe Text
    , gitCommitMessage :: Maybe Text
    , gitPush :: Bool
    , gitTag :: Maybe Text
    }
```

### 3. Define Import Options
```haskell
data ImportOptions = ImportOptions
    { importSource :: ImportSource        -- Where to import from
    , importDestination :: ImportDestination  -- Where to put imported configs
    , importNamespace :: Maybe Text       -- Filter by namespace
    , importMode :: ImportMode            -- How to handle conflicts
    }

data ImportSource
    = ImportFromFile FilePath             -- Archive file
    | ImportFromGit GitImportOptions      -- Git repository

data GitImportOptions = GitImportOptions
    { gitUrl :: Text
    , gitRef :: Maybe Text                -- branch/tag/commit
    , gitNamespace :: Maybe Text          -- specific namespace to import
    }

data ImportDestination
    = ImportToCurrentDir                  -- Import to current directory
    | ImportToPath FilePath               -- Import to specific path
    | ImportToConfigDir                   -- Import to ~/.config/agents-exe

data ImportMode
    = ImportFailOnConflict                -- Fail if files exist
    | ImportOverwrite                     -- Overwrite existing files
    | ImportMerge                         -- Merge configs (smart combine)
```

### 4. Command Parsers
Add parsers for the new commands:

```haskell
parseExportCommand :: Parser Command
parseExportCommand =
    Export <$> parseExportOptions

parseExportOptions :: Parser ExportOptions
parseExportOptions =
    ExportOptions
        <$> parseExportSource
        <*> parseExportDestination
        <*> optional parseArchiveFormat
        <*> optional parseNamespaceOption
        <*> parseIncludeToolsFlag
        <*> parseIncludeMcpFlag

parseImportCommand :: Parser Command
parseImportCommand =
    Import <$> parseImportOptions

-- Similar parsers for ImportOptions...

parseArchiveFormat :: Parser ArchiveFormat
parseArchiveFormat =
    flag' TarFormat (long "tar" <> help "Use tar format")
    <|> flag' TarGzFormat (long "tar.gz" <> help "Use tar.gz format")
    <|> flag' ZipFormat (long "zip" <> help "Use zip format")
```

### 5. Register Commands
Add to the subparser in `parseProgOptions`:
```haskell
<*> hsubparser
    ( command "check" (info parseCheckCommand (idm))
        <> command "tui" (info parseTuiChatCommand (idm))
        <> command "run" (info parseOneShotTextualCommand (idm))
        <> command "echo-prompt" (info parseEchoPromptCommand (idm))
        <> command "describe" (info parseSelfDescribeCommand (idm))
        <> command "init" (info parseInitializeCommand (idm))
        <> command "mcp-server" (info parseMcpServer (idm))
        <> command "session-print" (info parseSessionPrintCommand (progDesc "Print a session file"))
        <> command "export" (info parseExportCommand (progDesc "Export agent configurations"))
        <> command "import" (info parseImportCommand (progDesc "Import agent configurations"))
    )
```

### 6. Command Handlers
Add handlers in the `prog` function:
```haskell
prog pargs = do
    -- ... existing setup ...
    case pargs.mainCommand of
        -- ... existing cases ...
        Export opts -> handleExport opts pargs
        Import opts -> handleImport opts pargs

handleExport :: ExportOptions -> Prog -> IO ()
handleExport opts prog = do
    -- Load agents based on exportSource
    -- Build ExportPackage
    -- Export to destination (archive or git)
    putStrLn $ "Exported to: " ++ showDestination opts.exportDestination

handleImport :: ImportOptions -> Prog -> IO ()
handleImport opts prog = do
    -- Import from source
    -- Handle conflicts based on importMode
    -- Write to destination
    putStrLn $ "Imported to: " ++ showDestination opts.importDestination
```

### 7. Command Usage Examples
Document these usage patterns:

```bash
# Export current agent to tar.gz
agents-exe --agent-file ./my-agent.json export --output my-agent.tar.gz

# Export to zip with namespace
agents-exe export --agent-file ./agent.json --namespace team-a.project --output agent.zip --zip

# Export to git repo
agents-exe export --agent-file ./agent.json --git-url git@github.com:org/agents.git --git-push

# Export all agents
agents-exe export --all --output all-agents.tar.gz

# Import from archive
agents-exe import --from-file ./agent.tar.gz --to ./imported/

# Import from git (specific namespace)
agents-exe import --git-url https://github.com/org/agents.git --namespace team-a --to ./team-agents/

# Import with overwrite
agents-exe import --from-file agent.tar.gz --overwrite

# List available namespaces in git repo
agents-exe import --git-url https://github.com/org/agents.git --list-namespaces
```

### 8. Files to Modify
- **Modify**: `app/Main.hs` - Add Command constructors, parsers, handlers

## Trade-offs

**Command Naming**
- `export`/`import`: Standard, intuitive
- `pack`/`unpack`: Archive-centric
- `push`/`pull`: Git-centric
- **Decision**: Use `export`/`import` with format-specific flags

**Default Import Destination**
- Current directory: Explicit, user knows where files go
- Config directory (~/.config/agents-exe): Consistent with --agent-file default
- **Decision**: Default to current directory, allow --to-config-dir flag

**Namespace in Export vs Import**
- Export with namespace: Tags the package
- Import with namespace: Filters incoming
- **Decision**: Support both for flexibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `export` command added with all parsing options
- [ ] `import` command added with all parsing options
- [ ] Help text visible via `agents-exe export --help`
- [ ] All example commands from section 7 work
- [ ] Proper error messages for invalid combinations
- [ ] Integration with existing --agent-file flag for source selection

## Dependencies
- Depends on: All three previous issues (Types, Archive, Git)
- This is the integration point that ties everything together for CLI users
